content.verdict: The Four Seasons Resort Bora Bora is the most consistently excellent luxury property on the island — and for honeymooners, its adults-only policy is a decisive advantage. Positioned on a motu on the eastern side of the lagoon, it delivers the best Mount Otemanu views from overwater bungalows of any resort on Bora Bora, since the eastern lagoon position puts the volcano directly in front of you. Every overwater bungalow has its own plunge pool, which sets it apart from most competitors at this price point. Service quality is unmistakably Four Seasons: anticipatory, warm, and effortlessly competent. Book here if you want the best version of the classic Bora Bora honeymoon experience without compromise. The trade-off: it's motu-based, so any off-property exploration requires boat transfers, and the dining, while excellent, is expensive even by Bora Bora standards. Skip it if you're budget-conscious or prefer a larger resort with more activity variety. For couples who consider Bora Bora a once-in-a-lifetime trip and want the very best, this is the benchmark.
content.best_room: Book the Overwater Bungalow with Plunge Pool — every overwater room at the Four Seasons includes a private plunge pool, but request the Premier or Otemanu-view category specifically. The Otemanu-facing bungalows on the northwest arm of the pier have a direct sightline to the volcano from your plunge pool and deck. Request bungalow #500s for the best combination of Otemanu views and privacy. The Sunset Water Villa adds extra space and is worth the premium for a 7-night honeymoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Seaplane Arrival & First Plunge
itinerary_7_nights.description: The ultimate Bora Bora arrival: book the Air Tahiti Nui seaplane from Papeete for around $400 per person — the 45-minute flight arrives on the water at the resort's private dock in an experience that redefines arrival. If budget doesn't allow, the resort boat transfer from the airport takes 15 minutes. Settle into your bungalow, order champagne, and take your first plunge pool dip watching Otemanu glow at sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dawn Snorkel & Overwater Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Wake at 6am and step directly into the lagoon from your bungalow steps. The eastern motu position puts you close to some of the best coral in the lagoon — the Four Seasons' house reef has blacktip sharks and hawksbill turtles most mornings. Book the 'Te Ora' couples treatment at the overwater spa for 10am: a 90-minute ritual using Polynesian tamanu oil. Afternoon on the plunge pool deck with in-villa snacks.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lagoon Excursion & Ray Village
itinerary_7_nights.description: The resort's guided lagoon tour leaves at 9am by outrigger canoe. The circuit visits Motu Tapu, the renowned stingray-feeding site where rays swarm around your legs in knee-deep water, and a snorkel stop at a pristine coral garden. The Four Seasons guide is knowledgeable about the marine ecosystem. Return by 1pm. Afternoon: kayak from your bungalow dock, paddling to the motu's sand beach for an hour of total solitude.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Motu Tapu Private Picnic
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrange a private half-day charter to Motu Tapu through the concierge — the Four Seasons team sets this up impeccably with a packed picnic, snorkel gear, and champagne. The uninhabited islet is 20 minutes by boat. You'll likely be alone there for several hours. The snorkeling on the lagoon side is the best in the area. Return by 3pm. Evening: dinner at Fare Hoa restaurant, where the service and seafood are exceptional.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Diving Anau & Couples Cook Class
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning two-tank dive with the resort's PADI center to Anau — the site on the south side of the island where manta rays are regularly cleaned. Even snorkelers can join the surface-level portion. Back by noon. Afternoon: the resort offers a Polynesian cooking class in small groups where you learn to prepare poisson cru, coconut bread, and taro — a genuinely fun two-hour activity that makes a memorable honeymoon experience.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunset Sailing & Private Deck Dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Four Seasons' private catamaran sunset sail departs at 4:30pm. The circuit around the lagoon gives every classic postcard angle of Otemanu in golden light, with unlimited champagne and canapes on board. Return at dusk. Tonight: request the in-villa private dinner experience — the culinary team sets a decorated table on your plunge pool deck, lit with torches and tiare flowers, for a multi-course Polynesian menu served by your dedicated villa host.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Last Morning Ritual & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Four Seasons checkout is 12 noon; request an extension to 2pm. Spend your last morning doing exactly what you did on day one: dawn lagoon snorkel, plunge pool coffee, in-villa breakfast on the deck. The resort provides complimentary luggage storage so you can swim until the last moment. Boat transfer to airport takes 15 minutes. Seaplane return to Papeete is the ideal way to close the loop if you booked it on arrival.

honest_caveats: This is among the most expensive hotels in Bora Bora — rates regularly exceed $1,500 per night in peak season for the plunge pool categories, making a 7-night stay potentially a $12,000+ accommodation cost alone before food and activities.

Motu location means boat transfers every time you go off-property — no spontaneous town trips or restaurant-hopping on the main island. The self-contained nature is a feature for some couples and a limitation for others.

Peak season (July–August) brings families with teenagers despite the adults-only policy applying only to certain areas — the resort is adult-only overall but verify current policy at time of booking, as classifications can change.
Dining costs are at the very top of the Bora Bora scale. Budget at least $450–500 per couple per day for full resort dining. Breakfast alone runs $80–120 per person if not included.
content.hotel_email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon Stay – [Your Names] – Arriving [Check-in Date]

Dear Four Seasons Resort Bora Bora Reservations Team,

My partner and I are honeymooning at your resort from [check-in date] to [check-out date] (Booking reference: [XXXXXX]). We have booked an Overwater Bungalow with Plunge Pool and this is our honeymoon — we have been dreaming of this trip for a long time.

We would love your help with the following:

1. Please could we be assigned a bungalow with a direct Mount Otemanu view — ideally on the northwest arm of the pier, as far from the beach pavilion as possible for maximum privacy.
2. We would love to arrange the private in-villa dinner experience for our sixth evening ([date]) — decorated with tiare flowers, torchlit, with the full set menu on our plunge pool deck.
3. If there are any honeymoon welcome amenities you are able to arrange, they would make our arrival completely magical.

We cannot wait to celebrate with you.

Warm regards,
[Your Names]
[Contact number]

faqs.question: Is Four Seasons Bora Bora worth it for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: If budget allows, yes — it's the most consistent five-star property on the island. The adults-only policy, private plunge pools in every overwater bungalow, best Otemanu views from an eastern motu position, and signature Four Seasons service make this the benchmark honeymoon property in Bora Bora.

faqs.question: What overwater bungalow category should we book?
faqs.answer: Every overwater room includes a plunge pool — book the Premier Otemanu View category and request the northwest arm of the pier for the direct volcano sightline. The Sunset Water Villa is a meaningful step up in size and is worth considering for a 7-night honeymoon if budget allows.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October are the sweet spots: dry weather, calm lagoon, and 20–30% lower rates than peak July–August. The eastern motu position at the Four Seasons gets slightly more wind than the western properties — shoulder season offers calmer conditions and fewer guests.

faqs.question: How do you get to Four Seasons Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: Fly Papeete to Bora Bora Airport on Air Tahiti (50 minutes). The resort's private speedboat meets you at the airport pier — 15 minutes to the motu. For the ultimate arrival, book the Air Tahiti Nui seaplane from Papeete: a 45-minute flight that lands directly at the resort dock. Budget around $400 per person each way for the seaplane.

faqs.question: What does the honeymoon package include?
faqs.answer: Four Seasons' romance package typically includes daily breakfast, one couples spa treatment, a private dinner experience, and flower decoration on arrival. Current package contents vary by season — confirm at booking. Four Seasons Preferred Partner agents can add additional credits and upgrades.

faqs.question: One insider tip for honeymooners at Four Seasons Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: Book the seaplane arrival from Papeete. The 45-minute flight over the Society Islands and the final approach over the lagoon to the resort's private dock is a once-in-a-lifetime arrival experience. It costs around $400 per person each way but reframes the entire trip from the first moment.

true_cost_breakdown.item: 7 nights overwater bungalow with plunge pool
